scope: documentation/demo content only; not wired to backend seed or runtime

worlds.name: Nara Vale
worlds.world_statement: A threshold of projected bodies, soft architectures, and rooms that remember light.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A dark field, a suspended image, a name that feels like a signal, one line of copy, and a single Enter action.
worlds.public_identity: Experimental visual artist working with projection, fabric, skin, shadow, and architectural darkness.
worlds.proves: Presence can create emotional gravity before it explains anything.

worlds.name: Kira Stone
worlds.world_statement: Image systems for people who read the city as material.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A field of images at different scales where the city appears as texture, object, reflection, mark, sign, and threshold.
worlds.public_identity: Photographer and designer studying urban surfaces, signage, pavements, objects, and informal visual systems.
worlds.proves: Presence can compete with portfolio sites by making images feel spatial and curated.

worlds.name: Yorke Collier
worlds.world_statement: A record of exhibitions, essays, campaigns, and cultural systems built through language, image, and public trust.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A masthead, a thesis, numbered files, and evidence that feel like a cultural intelligence document.
worlds.public_identity: Curator, writer, and creative strategist working across exhibitions, campaigns, public programs, and cultural documentation.
worlds.proves: Presence can extend into curators, consultants, cultural strategists, and contract professionals without becoming LinkedIn.

worlds.name: Anika Wells Studio
worlds.world_statement: Clay, ash, vessel forms, and small rituals of repair.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A bench, a fragment, a vessel, a note, a hand, and a material surface that make process feel valuable.
worlds.public_identity: Ceramic and object practice focused on clay, ash, vessel forms, repair, domestic ritual, and the memory of use.
worlds.proves: Presence can turn process into value, which normal portfolio systems rarely do.

worlds.name: Mina River
worlds.mechanism: care pathway / method room / trust surface
worlds.world_statement: A grounded practice for people moving through grief, transition, and nervous-system repair.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A calm threshold, a clear method, and a soft but confident path through ways to work.
worlds.public_identity: Facilitator, somatic educator, and community practitioner supporting care, grief, transition, and embodied listening.
worlds.proves: Presence can make practitioner pages feel trustworthy and human without becoming generic wellness funnels.

worlds.name: Waratah Room
worlds.mechanism: public noticeboard / program wall / cultural node
worlds.world_statement: A room for sound, practice, gathering, and local cultural memory.
worlds.first_10_seconds: A room, a public invitation, program traces, and a sense of people gathering.
worlds.public_identity: Small cultural venue and collective room for sound, workshops, exhibitions, repair circles, youth programs, and public memory.
worlds.proves: Presence can hold venues and cultural organisations as public infrastructure, not brochures or event lists.
